Title: CmBr confers fruit bitterness under CPPU treatment in melon

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to cucurbitadienol, the triterpenoid scaffold precursor for cucurbitacin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with increased cucurbitacin B accumulation under CPPU; activated by CmBr and CmRSM1-associated regulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmBi was one of eight CuB structural genes strongly induced by CPPU during early fruit development. 2. Its expression remained responsive in ivf05IL(CmBr) but not in ivf05IL(Cmbr-2), indicating dependence on functional CmBr. 3. CmRSM1 overexpression increased CmBi expression together with higher CuB content.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; co-expression analysis; response comparison in CmBr introgression lines; transient CmRSM1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in CPPU-treated fruit, especially at 3 DAA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in CPPU-treated fruit, especially at 3 DAA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit (young fruit and mature fruit); also roots discussed for CuB accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Confers bitterness and mediates plant interaction with biotic or abiotic factors
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 20 mg/L CPPU treatment sprayed on ovaries at anthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary target for pathway engineering, though the paper emphasizes upstream regulators CmBr/CmRSM1 and CmBr editing for breeding
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a previously reported CuB biosynthetic gene and used here mainly as a downstream structural target rather than the primary experimental focus.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative tailoring steps of cucurbitacin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative modification steps in cucurbitacin B biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive association with CPPU-induced cucurbitacin B accumulation as a CmBr-activated structural gene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cm160 is one of the eight structural genes co-induced with CuB accumulation under CPPU. 2. Its induction depends on functional CmBr in introgression lines. 3. It is part of the downstream biosynthetic program activated during bitterness formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; co-expression analysis; expression comparison in CmBr functional vs loss-of-function lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in CPPU-treated fruit, especially at 3 DAA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in CPPU-treated fruit, especially at 3 DAA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit; roots discussed for CuB accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Confers bitterness and stress-related ecological function
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible pathway engineering target, but not primary breeding target in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific catalytic position not resolved in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative tailoring steps of cucurbitacin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative modification steps in cucurbitacin B biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports cucurbitacin B accumulation as part of the CmBr-responsive biosynthetic gene set under CPPU
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cm170 was co-expressed with other CuB structural genes in the turquoise module. 2. It was induced by CPPU in functional CmBr lines. 3. This induction was absent or reduced in CmBr loss-of-function introgression lines.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; co-expression analysis; comparative expression in ILs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit; roots discussed for CuB accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Confers bitterness and likely defense-related ecological roles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream structural gene, not directly functionally edited in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative tailoring steps of cucurbitacin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative modification steps in cucurbitacin B biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive structural contributor to CPPU-induced cucurbitacin B accumulation downstream of CmBr
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cm180 is one of the CuB biosynthetic genes in the turquoise module. 2. Its expression increases under CPPU in wild-type/functional CmBr backgrounds. 3. It remains unresponsive in Cmbr mutant introgression lines.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; co-expression analysis; comparative expression analysis in ILs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit; roots discussed for CuB accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Confers bitterness and mediates plant-environment interaction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific enzymatic step not dissected in this work.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative tailoring steps of cucurbitacin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative modification steps in cucurbitacin B biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the activated structural program required for high CuB accumulation under CPPU
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cm710 expression is elevated by CPPU in developing fruit. 2. Functional CmBr is required for this induction in introgression lines. 3. It is among the previously reported CmBt/CmBr-responsive CuB pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; comparative expression analysis; prior pathway assignment referenced in paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit; roots discussed for CuB accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned previously as activated by CmBt; in this paper treated mainly as downstream target of CmBr.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative tailoring steps of cucurbitacin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative modification steps in cucurbitacin B biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports CPPU-induced cucurbitacin B accumulation as a CmBr-responsive pathway gene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cm890 was induced by CPPU alongside other CuB structural genes. 2. Induction depended on functional CmBr in introgression lines. 3. It was also noted in previous work as a CmBt target, but here is included within the CmBr-activated pathway set.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; comparative expression analysis in ILs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit; roots discussed for CuB accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream target, not directly validated biochemically in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative tailoring steps of cucurbitacin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative modification steps in cucurbitacin B biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes positively to CuB biosynthesis as part of the CPPU- and CmBr-responsive pathway gene set
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cm490 was included among the eight structural genes induced by CPPU. 2. Its activation required functional CmBr. 3. It participates in the biosynthetic program leading to bitterness-associated CuB accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; comparative expression analysis in ILs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit; roots discussed for CuB accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific oxidative step not assigned in this article.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes acetylation of cucurbitacin pathway intermediate(s) leading toward cucurbitacin B formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product or immediate late intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with CuB accumulation as a late structural gene activated by CmBr under CPPU
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmACT was among the eight CuB biosynthetic genes induced by CPPU. 2. Its activation depended on a functional CmBr allele. 3. It represents a late tailoring step in the pathway linked to bitterness formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; comparative expression analysis in ILs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit; roots discussed for CuB accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential metabolic engineering target but not the breeding focus here
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Listed as previously reported CuB structural gene; used here as downstream readout of CmBr activity.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: P147 donor allele; ivf05 functional allele context
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 38; ivf05; P147; WT F1; Cmbr-1; ivf05IL(CmBr); ivf05IL(Cmbr-2)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit, especially young fruit under CPPU; roots also accumulate CuB
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Confers fruit bitterness and participates in plant responses to biotic/abiotic factors
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates eight CuB biosynthetic genes: CmBi; Cm160; Cm170; Cm180; Cm710; Cm890; Cm490; CmACT
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CmBi; Cm160; Cm170; Cm180; Cm710; Cm890; Cm490; CmACT
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional CmBr is essential for CPPU-induced cucurbitacin B accumulation and fruit bitterness; loss of function greatly reduces CuB and bitter fruit incidence
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major regulator and essential positive regulator for CPPU-induced CuB biosynthesis in melon fruit
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmBr showed the strongest induction among candidate bHLH factors, with ~22-fold upregulation in CPPU-treated fruit at 3 DAA. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 loss-of-function mutant Cmbr-1 and introgression mutant Cmbr-2 both largely abolished CPPU-induced CuB accumulation and bitterness. 3. Transcriptome analyses showed that functional CmBr is required for activation of eight CuB biosynthetic genes under CPPU. 4. Introgression of the nonfunctional Cmbr allele into elite ivf05 strongly reduced CPPU-induced bitter fruit without obvious penalties in fruit weight or SSC.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA/co-expression analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; introgression line validation; LC-MS metabolite quantification; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in young fruit (DAF 0, 2, 4 d) and root; low in stem and leaf; strongly induced by CPPU at 3 DAA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in young fruit and root; strongly induced by CPPU at 3 DAA
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Not experimentally confirmed; discussion suggests possible heterodimeric interaction with CmBt may be worth investigating
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CmRSM1 (upstream regulator); CmBt (possible but not validated interaction)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 20 mg/L CPPU treatment sprayed on ovaries at anthesis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CmBr promoter in melon; 2 kb promoter used in Y1H/LUC/EMSA studies as target of CmRSM1
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or introgressing nonfunctional Cmbr alleles can eliminate CPPU-induced fruit bitterness in elite melon with little apparent effect on fruit weight and soluble solids; strong breeding value for high-quality non-bitter cultivars under CPPU use
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant effect on fruit weight or flesh/placenta SSC reported; slight increase in fruit shape index may reflect developmental effects or donor background
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper proposes CmBr as the major regulator of CPPU-induced CuB biosynthesis, stronger in responsiveness than previously known CmBt.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB-related transcription factor with single SANT/MYB DNA-binding domain; RSM family
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: ivf05 context for transient overexpression; melon homolog of Arabidopsis RSM1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit; hairy roots in validation system
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Confers bitterness and participates in plant-environment interaction
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional regulation of cucurbitacin B biosynthesis via activation of CmBr
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates CmBr by binding the Myb motif in its promoter; indirectly increases CmBi expression and CuB accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases CuB accumulation in melon fruit and hairy roots by activating CmBr
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent upstream activator linking CPPU response to CmBr-mediated CuB biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmRSM1 was identified from the CPPU-responsive network as a MYB-related factor highly correlated with CmBr. 2. CmRSM1 is highly expressed in young fruit and is induced in the CPPU regulatory context. 3. Transient overexpression in fruit and hairy roots increased CuB content. 4. Y1H, dual-luciferase and EMSA showed that CmRSM1 directly binds the Myb motif in the CmBr promoter and activates CmBr transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient fruit overexpression; hairy root overexpression; CuB metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in young fruit (DAF 0, 2, 4 d); low in root, stem and leaf
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in young fruit; low in root, stem and leaf
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CPPU treatment context; transient infiltration at 10 DAA for validation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S-like overexpression vector context not explicitly stated; CmBr native promoter used as reporter target
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream engineering node for controlling bitterness, though paper emphasizes CmBr loss-of-function as the practical breeding route
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a MYB-related RSM family member not previously associated with terpenoid regulation in this lineage.
